steps.label: A
steps.intro: Let's start with the vowel A.
steps.narration: Notice the curve — the tail lifts from the bottom-right. Take your time and study the shape before you draw.
steps.buttyTip: When you're ready, tap Got it to continue.

steps.prompt: Type the romanization of this glyph.
steps.successFeedback: Correct. A is the foundation of Baybayin.

steps.label: E / I
steps.intro: E and I share one glyph in Baybayin.
steps.narration: It's a mirrored curve — the same base as A, opposite direction.
steps.buttyTip: Two vowels, one shape. That's vowel pairing.

steps.label: E / I
steps.successFeedback: Good. The mirrored curve is right.

steps.label: O / U
steps.intro: O and U also share a glyph — a clean circle.
steps.narration: Close the circle with a single, deliberate stroke.
steps.buttyTip: A clean close is the mark of a solid O / U.

steps.label: O / U
steps.prompt: Type either romanization for this glyph.
steps.successFeedback: Tama! Either answer works — it's vowel pairing.
